statusLine: EU fuel security remains under strain with 21 of 27 countries holding critical-level reserves in at least one fuel type, while oil prices have surged 29.9% since February's Middle East escalation.
fullAnalysis:
European fuel security faces significant pressure across the continent, with critical stock shortages affecting the majority of member states. Of 27 EU countries, 21 are operating with critical reserves in at least one fuel category, indicating widespread depletion following strategic reserve releases amid Middle East supply disruptions. Luxembourg, Poland, Austria, Romania, and Portugal face the most severe challenges, with critical levels across all three fuel types.

The aviation fuel situation is particularly concerning, with 18 of 27 countries holding critical jet fuel reserves below the 90-day minimum. Diesel supplies are similarly strained, with 16 countries operating at critical levels. Only petrol shows relative stability, though 13 countries still maintain critical petrol reserves. Nordic and Baltic states demonstrate stronger reserve positions, with Estonia, Latvia, and Ireland maintaining safe levels across most categories.

Oil market pressures compound these supply challenges, with Brent crude climbing to $94.17 per barrel—a 29.9% surge since the late-February Middle East escalation began. This price increase translates directly to EU fuel costs, with average petrol reaching €1.812 per liter and diesel at €1.821 per liter. Regional price disparities remain significant, ranging from Malta's €1.34 petrol to Denmark's €2.392 per liter.
keyPoints: 21 of 27 EU countries maintain critical fuel reserves in at least one category, with Luxembourg, Poland, Austria, Romania, and Portugal most severely affected across all fuel types
Aviation fuel faces the deepest shortages, with 18 of 27 countries below the mandatory 90-day minimum for jet fuel reserves
Brent crude oil has surged 29.9% to $94.17/barrel since February's Middle East crisis escalation, driving EU fuel costs to €1.812/L for petrol and €1.821/L for diesel
Nordic and Baltic states show strongest resilience with Estonia, Latvia, and Ireland maintaining adequate reserves, while Southern and Eastern European countries face the most acute shortages
EU-wide diesel reserves average 88.8 days—just below the 90-day safety threshold—while petrol maintains 96.3 days and jet fuel holds 87.3 days
